summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a high‑energy post‑coma show with Jerry’s standout vocals on Cumberland Blues and the rare mid‑set Hey Pocky Way.
review: The recording is praised as top‑notch, crisp and well‑balanced, delivering an enjoyable listening experience. Musically the concert shines thanks to Jerry Garcia’s vocal highlight on “Cumberland Blues,” Phil Lesh’s dancing bass lines, and a rare appearance of “Hey Pocky Way” that keeps the energy fired up from start to finish.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high star ratings (mostly 5) and enthusiastic comments about both performance and sound quality; no significant negatives reported.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Multiple reviewers call the sound “top‑notch” and “crisp”; no complaints about dropouts, muddiness or imbalance.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set is described as high‑energy from start to finish with a rare mid‑set inclusion that adds excitement; overall pacing and song selection are praised.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for strong, familiar guitar tone and especially his vocal performance on “Cumberland Blues”.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass lines described as “dancing on air” and in top form right from the start.
band_member_comments.Bob: Noted that he sings only a fragment of the first verse of a song, but no criticism of his playing overall.
